observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant identify the discrepancies between the reciprocal accounts, Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds, reconcile the same, and effect the necessary adjustments, as warranted, to reflect the correct account balances for fair presentation of accounts in the financial statements.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Accountant properly present prior period errors and adjustments by restating the prior year balances of the affected accounts shown as corresponding figures for the current year FS in compliance with the requirements under Paragraphs 46 to 53 of IPSAS 3.

Further, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare appropriate disclosures in the Notes to FS pertaining to prior period errors and adjustments pursuant to Paragraph 54 of IPSAS 3.

We recommended that the Municipal Assessor and the General Services Officer, in coordination with concerned national government agencies, facilitate the registration and transfer of titles of the subject parcels of land including those where the municipal site is presently located and those where municipal buildings and other structures were already constructed to ensure ownership and to fully protect the interest of the LGU.

We recommended that Management:

a) 	Prepare and submit the PIF as basis for the assessment of general insurance coverage over all insurable assets, property and interests of the LGU with the GIF of the GSIS; and

b) 	Ensure that the identified assets, property and interests are insured with the GIF of the GSIS to protect the LGU against the risks of non-indemnification in case of damage to or loss of property.

a) . . .

d) 	Repost immediately the damaged project signboard and update the project status as of a given date.

We recommended that Management:

a) 	Grant contract time extensions after careful examination of the complete and sufficient documents such as, but not limited to, the Monthly Time Suspension Report for contract time extensions due to unfavorable weather condition, and the certification from appropriate government agencies for contract time extensions due to shortage or non-availability of materials as provided under DPWH DO No. 42 s.2020, and Annex “E” of the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184, respectively;

b) 	Require contractors to post performance securities with complete details as to its period of validity and/or expiry; and

c) 	Require contractors to cause the extension of their performance securities corresponding the contract time extensions granted to them.

We recommended that Management thru the Municipal Engineer:

a) 	Inform the Office of the Auditor within ten (10) days after the award of the infrastructure project that the appropriate project signboards are already posted; 

b) 	Provide complete information as to the project duration, starting date of the project, target date of completion and status of the project in the project signboards to promote transparency and accountability; and

c) 	For ongoing infrastructure projects without project signboards, immediately post the project signboards in the project sites/locations. Henceforth, comply with the posting of project signboards for all infrastructure projects.

We recommended the following:

a) 	The Management review its organizational structure, the functions of each office/department, and its procedures in the preparation of contracts/purchase orders and the subsequent submission of copies of the same to the Office of the Auditor, which should be coursed thru the General Services Office instead of the Office of the Municipal Treasurer;

b) 	The General Services Officer furnish the Office of the Auditor with copies of contracts/purchase orders relative to the procurement of goods, services and infrastructure projects, including the relevant supporting documents, within five (5) days after execution to facilitate timely review and evaluation of the same; and

c) 	The General Services Officer submit notice of deliveries/completion to the Office of the Auditor relative to procured goods/infrastructure projects within twenty-four (24) hours after acceptance to facilitate the conduct of inspection.

Management keep abreast with the latest guidelines affecting PPE and semi-expendable properties, and direct the Municipal Accountant and the General Services Officer to comply with the pertinent provisions of COA Circular No. 2022-004 dated May 31, 2022 particularly with the preparation and maintenance of prescribed forms, registries, and reports to strengthen control over the LGU’s semi-expendable properties.

Make representations with identified source agencies for the return of unutilized balances related to completed projects and/or revert the same to the National Treasury or the unappropriated surplus in the general fund of the LGU, as warranted.

Properly monitor the withholding and remittance of mandatory contributions so as to avoid under/over-remittance of contributions; and review thoroughly the schedule of payables due to concerned agencies during the month and that remittance be based on actual contributions vis-a-vis payroll deductions.

Recommendation:
We recommended that Management thru the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Budget Officer:
Strictly comply with the transfer of unexpended balances of LDRRMF to the STF on the basis of the 5 per cent allocation of the estimated revenue and the original appropriations reflected in the annual budget and not against the actual realized income/revenue for the year;
Revisit the existing policies and procedures in setting up the estimated revenue in the annual budget, as basis for the 5 per cent allocation for LDRRMF, to avoid overestimation of revenues, which could result to the incurrence of actual realized income/revenue below the estimated revenue; and
Transfer the amount of P209,632.76 from the GF to the STF pertaining to the shortage (net) of the unexpended balances of LDRRMF actually transferred to the STF for CYs 2019 to 2022, to be used by the LGU for the purpose of supporting its disaster risk reduction and management activities.

Recommendation:
We recommended that:
The Local Finance Committee identity and create new/additional source of local revenue to finance the implementation of various programs, projects and activities;
The Local Finance Committee set up an annual estimated total revenue that is realistic and attainable in order for Management not to incur shortfall or deficit in its actual revenue collections; and
The Municipal Treasurer devise measures/strategies to strengthen and improve the LGU’s collection efficiency in order to meet the LGU’s revenue targets.

Recommendation:

We recommended that Management:

a) 	Direct the LDRRM Council to conduct proper planning and ensure that only disaster risk reduction and management activities are included in its LDRRMF Investment Plan and the annual budget for the 5 per cent LDRRMF. In relation thereto, funding for administrative expenses, Maintenance and Other Operating Expenses (MOOE) and Capital Outlay (CO) requirements of the LDRRM Office be included in the appropriations of said office under the Office of the Municipal Mayor; and

b) 	Refrain from charging expenditures against LDRRMF which are not directly related to the four thematic areas of disaster risk reduction and management activities namely, disaster prevention and mitigation, disaster preparedness, disaster response and disaster rehabilitation and recovery.

We recommended the following:

c) 	The Management consider maintaining only one depository account in the Trust Fund where the unexpended balances of the LDRRMF are transferred and initiate the closure of the other subject depository account after transferring the balances thereof to the depository account which will be retained/maintained.

We reiterated the following audit recommendations:

a) 	The Management ensure timely implementation of infrastructure projects thru effective monitoring of the performance of contractors; adopt calibrated measures as provided under GPPB Circular No. 03-2019 dated 8 March 2019 particularly when contractors incur negative slippage during the contract duration;

b) 	The Municipal Engineer prepare the Statement of Time Elapsed showing the status of project implementation with regards to the contract duration and attach the same to every progress and final billings of contractors; and

c) 	The Municipal Accountant, with the assistance of the Municipal Engineer, exert effort in the computation of liquidated damages arising from the delays incurred by contractors in the implementation of infrastructure projects, and deduct/collect the same from the retention money, other securities posted by contractors, or from any money due or which may become due the contractors, whichever is available to the Management.

We recommended that the BAC and TWG diligently verify, validate and ascertain all statements made and documents submitted by bidders in conducting post-qualification, and disqualify future bids for any deficiencies discovered such as the commitment/provision of same key personnel and equipment to projects with same or overlapping project duration.

We recommended that the BAC and TWG carefully conduct post-qualification of bids, and disqualify future bids of contractors with unsatisfactory performance in its ongoing government and/or private projects pursuant to Section 34.3.b.ii of the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184.

We recommended that Management adhere to the provisions in the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 pertaining to the imposition of warranty securities in the form of retention money or special bank guarantee for the delivered and accepted goods, in order to protect not only the interest of the government but also those project beneficiaries and other third parties involved in the execution of government projects.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer strictly require from the supplier of goods the issuance of sales invoice as principal evidence, and attach the same to the disbursement vouchers for submission to the Office of the Auditor.

We recommended that Management, thru its GAD Focal Point System, establish and maintain a GAD database containing sex-disaggregated data and other gender-related information which shall serve as basis in conducting gender analysis, identifying gender issues and determining the appropriate programs, projects and activities that would address the identified gender issues to be incorporated in the GAD Plan and Budget. In compliance thereto, Management may consider the allocation of budget in its GAD Plan and Budget for expenditures relative to the establishment of GAD database.

We recommended that Management include in its GAD Plan and Budget the attendance of the members of the GAD Focal Point System to capability trainings/seminars to capacitate themselves in the conduct of gender analysis and gender audit through the use of various gender assessment tools. Henceforth, the GFPS institutionalize the conduct of gender analysis and gender audit through the use of gender assessment tools.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
Reconcile the noted variances between the balances per books and per bank, and record the necessary adjustments in the books of accounts, as warranted; and
Present the current and non-current portion of the Loans Payable – Domestic account in the Statement of Financial Position and the Notes to Financial Statements for fair presentation of the account in the financial statements.

Recommendation:
We recommended that Management:
Conduct verification, analysis, and validation of the balances of the Due to NGAs accounts, determine the names of the source agencies and the status of programs, projects and activities for which funds were received; and maintain complete records of the same;
Direct the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the balances of Due to NGAs account with the balances of concerned source agencies, and effect the necessary adjustments pursuant to IPSAS 23, as warranted; and
Make representations with identified source agencies for the return of unutilized balances related to completed projects and/or remit the same to the unappropriated surplus in the general fund of the LGU, as warranted.

Recommendation:
We recommended the following:
The Municipal Treasurer transfer all interests earned and subsequent interests, if any, from depository bank accounts maintained under the Trust Fund to the General Fund; and
The Municipal Accountant prepare the necessary adjustments in the books of accounts relative to the transfer of all interests earned from the Trust Fund to the General Fund; and henceforth, properly account subsequent interests, if any, as interest income in the books of accounts.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant effect the necessary adjustments in the books of accounts, as applicable, and carefully review and record financial transactions using the appropriate accounts in accordance with the Revised Chart of Accounts for Local Government Units.

Recommendation:
We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Accountant properly present prior period errors and adjustments by restating the prior year balances of the affected accounts shown as corresponding figures for the current year FS in compliance with the requirements under Paragraphs 46 to 53 of IPSAS 3.
Further, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare appropriate disclosures in the Notes to FS pertaining to prior period errors and adjustments pursuant to Paragraph 54 of IPSAS 3.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Budget Officer coordinate in the preparation of an accurate and complete SCBAA, taking into consideration the original budgeted amounts in the annual budget, the amounts of supplemental budgets, if any, and the balances of all continuing appropriations. Also, the Municipal Accountant present proper disclosure in the Notes to Financial Statements for any changes/material differences between the original and final budget.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant provide complete and proper disclosures in the Notes to Financial Statements pertaining to the amounts and details of the unexpended balances of the LDRRMF for the current and prior years, showing the amounts available for the year, the amounts utilized during the year and the balances at year-end.

Recommendation:
We recommended that Management determine the necessity and authority of maintaining the dormant cash in bank accounts and revert the same to the unappropriated surplus in the general fund, if warranted, to be made available for appropriation to support other local government programs and projects.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
Prepare and maintain the RAWO to record and monitor the dormant unliquidated cash advance amounting to P270,000.00 which were written off; and
Prepare the appropriate disclosures in the Notes to FS relative to the accounts written off.

Recommendation:
We recommended that Management take full opportunity on the application of COA Circular No. 2020-006 and fully comply with the prescribed guidelines and procedures, particularly the conduct of physical count of PPE, the recognition of PPE items found at station, the disposition for non-existing/missing PPE items, the procedures in the derecognition of non-existing/missing PPEs without available record of accountability, and the recording of accounting entries and disclosures in the notes to financial statements, for the purpose of one-time cleansing of PPE account balances of the LGU

Recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Assessor and the General Services Officer, in coordination with concerned national government agencies, facilitate the registration and transfer of titles of the subject parcels of land including those where the municipal site is presently located and those where municipal buildings and other structures were already constructed to ensure ownership and to fully protect the interest of the LGU.

Recommendation:
We recommended the following:
The Property Unit/General Services Officer prepare the IIRUP in its prescribed form for all unserviceable property, and the WMR for all waste materials, subject for disposal;
The Appraisal Committee determine the appraised value of unserviceable property subject to disposal; and
The General Services Officer dispose the unserviceable property and supplies in accordance with the rules and regulations on supply and property management in local government units.

Recommendation:
We recommended that Management:
Prepare and submit the PIF as basis for the assessment of general insurance coverage over all insurable assets, property and interests of the LGU with the GIF of the GSIS; and
Ensure that the identified assets, property and interests are insured with the GIF of the GSIS to protect the LGU against the risks of non-indemnification in case of damage to or loss of property.

Recommendation:
We recommended that Management:
Direct the LDRRM Council to conduct proper planning and ensure that only disaster risk reduction and management activities are included in its LDRRMF Investment Plan and the annual budget for the 5 per cent LDRRMF. In relation thereto, funding for administrative expenses, Maintenance and Other Operating Expenses (MOOE) and Capital Outlay (CO) requirements of the LDRRM Office be included in the appropriations of said office under the Office of the Municipal Mayor; and
Refrain from charging expenditures against LDRRMF which are not directly related to the four thematic areas of disaster risk reduction and management activities namely, disaster prevention and mitigation, disaster preparedness, disaster response and disaster rehabilitation and recovery.

Recommendation:
We recommended the following:
The Municipal Accountant and Municipal Budget Officer properly monitor the disbursements relative to the LDRRMF based on the actual source of funds, that is General Fund or Special Trust Fund, to avoid disbursements erroneously charged against both funds;
The Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer immediately process the transfer of funds from the Trust Fund to the General Fund equivalent to the amount of P2.157 million, duly approved by the Local Chief Executive; and
The Management consider maintaining only one depository account in the Trust Fund where the unexpended balances of the LDRRMF are transferred and initiate the closure of the other subject depository account after transferring the balances thereof to the depository account which will be retained/maintained.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the LDRRM Officer prepare the LDRRMFIP in accordance with the prescribed format as provided in COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.

Recommendation:
We recommended the following:
The Management, thru its HRMO, consider adding a stipulation in the contracts of concerned JO/COS personnel their authority to go on official travel and claim traveling expenses;
The Local Chief Executive issue a certification pursuant to Item B.5 of COA Resolution No. 2021-044 for official travel of JO/COS personnel, in the absence of stipulation in their contracts authorizing them to go on official travel and claim traveling expenses; and
The Municipal Accountant carefully review the supporting documents pertaining to claims of JO/COS personnel for traveling expenses and ensure that such claims are supported with the required certification from the Local Chief Executive or his authorized representative.

Recommendation:
We reiterated the following audit recommendations:
The Management ensure timely implementation of infrastructure projects thru effective monitoring of the performance of contractors; adopt calibrated measures as provided under GPPB Circular No. 03-2019 dated 8 March 2019 particularly when contractors incur negative slippage during the contract duration;
The Municipal Engineer prepare the Statement of Time Elapsed showing the status of project implementation with regards to the contract duration and attach the same to every progress and final billings of contractors; and
The Municipal Accountant, with the assistance of the Municipal Engineer, exert effort in the computation of liquidated damages arising from the delays incurred by contractors in the implementation of infrastructure projects, and deduct/collect the same from the retention money, other securities posted by contractors, or from any money due or which may become due the contractors, whichever is available to the Management.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Engineer:
Submit immediately the required additional documents and detailed justifications/ explanations/ computations relative to the deficiencies and inconsistencies noted in the Initial Technical Evaluation Report; and
Exercise due prudence in the preparation of the Program of Works and Approved Budget for the Contract of infrastructure projects as well as the review of the bid estimates submitted by contractors.

Recommendation:
We recommended that Management:
Grant contract time extensions after careful examination of the complete and sufficient documents such as, but not limited to, the Monthly Time Suspension Report for contract time extensions due to unfavorable weather condition, and the certification from appropriate government agencies for contract time extensions due to shortage or non-availability of materials as provided under DPWH DO No. 42 s.2020, and Annex “E” of the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184, respectively;
Require contractors to post performance securities with complete details as to its period of validity and/or expiry; and
Require contractors to cause the extension of their performance securities corresponding the contract time extensions granted to them.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the BAC and TWG diligently verify, validate and ascertain all statements made and documents submitted by bidders in conducting post-qualification, and disqualify future bids for any deficiencies discovered such as the commitment/provision of same key personnel and equipment to projects with same or overlapping project duration.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the BAC and TWG carefully conduct post-qualification of bids, and disqualify future bids of contractors with unsatisfactory performance in its ongoing government and/or private projects pursuant to Section 34.3.b.ii of the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184.

Recommendation:
We recommended that Management adhere to the provisions in the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 pertaining to the imposition of warranty securities in the form of retention money or special bank guarantee for the delivered and accepted goods, in order to protect not only the interest of the government but also those project beneficiaries and other third parties involved in the execution of government projects.

Recommendation:
We recommended that Management thru the Municipal Engineer:
Inform the Office of the Auditor within ten (10) days after the award of the infrastructure project that the appropriate project signboards are already posted;
Provide complete information as to the project duration, starting date of the project, target date of completion and status of the project in the project signboards to promote transparency and accountability; and
For ongoing infrastructure projects without project signboards, immediately post the project signboards in the project sites/locations. Henceforth, comply with the posting of project signboards for all infrastructure projects.

Recommendation:
We recommended the following:
The Management review its organizational structure, the functions of each office/department, and its procedures in the preparation of contracts/purchase orders and the subsequent submission of copies of the same to the Office of the Auditor, which should be coursed thru the General Services Office instead of the Office of the Municipal Treasurer;
The General Services Officer furnish the Office of the Auditor with copies of contracts/purchase orders relative to the procurement of goods, services and infrastructure projects, including the relevant supporting documents, within five (5) days after execution to facilitate timely review and evaluation of the same; and
The General Services Officer submit notice of deliveries/completion to the Office of the Auditor relative to procured goods/infrastructure projects within twenty-four (24) hours after acceptance to facilitate the conduct of inspection

Recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer strictly require from the supplier of goods the issuance of sales invoice as principal evidence, and attach the same to the disbursement vouchers for submission to the Office of the Auditor.

Recommendation:
We recommended that Management, thru its GAD Focal Point System, establish and maintain a GAD database containing sex-disaggregated data and other gender-related information which shall serve as basis in conducting gender analysis, identifying gender issues and determining the appropriate programs, projects and activities that would address the identified gender issues to be incorporated in the GAD Plan and Budget. In compliance thereto, Management may consider the allocation of budget in its GAD Plan and Budget for expenditures relative to the establishment of GAD database.

Recommendation:
We recommended that Management include in its GAD Plan and Budget the attendance of the members of the GAD Focal Point System to capability trainings/seminars to capacitate themselves in the conduct of gender analysis and gender audit through the use of various gender assessment tools. Henceforth, the GFPS institutionalize the conduct of gender analysis and gender audit through the use of gender assessment tools.

Recommendation:
We recommended that Management:
Direct the GFPS to conduct proper GAD planning and budgeting and ensure that only GAD-related programs, projects and activities are included in its GAD Plan and Budget; and
Refrain from charging expenditures against the GAD Fund which are not directly related to the purpose for which the fund is established.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the General Services Officer exert effort to trace prior years’ records and determine the balances of Fuel, Oil and Lubricants and Breeding Stocks accounts amounting to P70,877.20 and 260,400.00, respectively; and gather pertinent documents showing the consumption, transfer or other disposal of fuel, oil and lubricants, and the transfer, sale, loss or death of breeding stocks. Also, the Municipal Accountant prepare the necessary adjusting entries, as warranted.
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant submit its monthly trial balance and quarterly financial statements along with the required reports and supporting schedules within the prescribed period.

We recommended that Management:

a)	Effect the full utilization of the LDRRMF in order to strengthen the LGU’s disaster preparedness; and

b)	Direct the LDRRMO together with the members of the LDRRMC to identify and incorporate in the LDRRMFIP the PPAs to be funded from the previous years' unexpended QRF and DRRMF-MOOE which were transferred to the Special Trust Fund.

We recommended that the BAC comply with the pertinent provisions under the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 and GPPB Circular No. 02-2020 particularly on the preparation of the APP and changes made thereto, the PMR to include all procurement activities undertaken thru competitive bidding and other alternative modes of procurement, and the APCPI Results. Henceforth, electronically submit these reports to the GPPB within the prescribed deadlines. Further, copies of these reports be furnished the Office of the Auditor for monitoring and as basis for auditor’s action, if any.

We recommended that Management:

a)	Prepare and issue the NOA to winning bidders, not only for the procurement of goods and services undertaken thru competitive bidding, but also for other alternative modes of procurement; and

b)	Direct the BAC to post the NOA, Purchase Order, including Notice to Proceed, if necessary, in the PhilGEPS website, the LGU website, if available, and at any conspicuous places in the premises of the LGU, for procurement of goods and services with ABC of above P50,000.00.
We recommended that the BAC comply with the existing rules and regulations of the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 particularly on the approval of BAC Resolutions by the Head of Procuring Entity.

We recommended that the BAC comply with the proper and timely posting of RFQs in the PhilGEPS website relative to the procurement of goods and services with ABC of above P50,000.00 undertaken thru Small Value Procurement, as prescribed under Annex “H” of the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant comply with the prescribed documentary requirements particularly the as-built plans relative to completed infrastructure projects as required under COA Circular No. 2012-001 before claims are processed and paid.

We recommended that Management require the concerned officers and employees constituting the BAC, TWG and BAC Secretariat to refund the excess amounts of honoraria received by them. Henceforth, Management to comply with the applicable laws, rules and regulations in the payment of honoraria to government personnel involved in the procurement taking into consideration the maximum allowable rate for each completed procurement project.

We recommended that Management:

a)	Observe and implement the correct employer’s share on the monthly premium contributions to the HDMF, which should not exceed P100.00 per month per employee; and

b)	Make representations with the HDMF to consider the over-remittance of employer’s share as advance payment on the premium contributions of the Municipal Government.

We recommended that Management comply with the prescribed threshold of P5,000.00 for the minimum net take home pay that each employee should receive; refrain from approving additional loans which may reduce the net take home pay of employees below said threshold; and orient all employees of the provision on the net take home pay as provided every year in the General Provisions of the GAA.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive and the Legislative Department coordinate for the formulation and establishment of internal guidelines on the granting of burial, medical and other financial assistance for the proper and efficient utilization of the AICS Program.
(b) existing GAD Code is not updated to include the mandate of the Magna Carta for Women

     8. We recommended that Management:

Recommendation:

     5. We recommended the following:

          b) Require the Municipal Accountant to present in  the  Notes  to

             Report of Local Road Network, and to prepare and maintain  the
             Local Road Network Property Cards for each local road.

Recommendation:

     7. We recommended that the Municipal Accountant exert effort in tracing
        back prior years’ records pertaining to acquisition and/or recording
        of the various land improvements amounting to P1.414 million.


     8. Further, we recommended that the  Municipal  Accountant  effect  the
        necessary adjustments  for  the  recognition  of  the  prior  years’
        depreciation and the corresponding accumulated depreciation of Other

        month of  purchase/completion,  and useful  life.  Thereafter,  the
        Municipal  Accountant  provide  for  its  annual  depreciation,   if
        applicable.

Recommendation:

     9. We  recommended  that  the  Municipal  Accountant  and  the  General
        Services Officer exert effort to  trace  prior  years’  records  and
        determine the balances of Fuel,  Oil  and  Lubricants  and  Breeding
        Stocks   accounts   amounting   to   P70,877.20   and    260,400.00,

        consumption, transfer or other disposal of fuel, oil and lubricants,
        and the transfer, sale, loss or death of breeding stocks. Also,  the
        Municipal Accountant prepare the  necessary  adjusting  entries,  as
        warranted.

Recommendation:

    10. We recommended the following:


          a) The Municipal Accountant  conduct  analysis  of  the  Accounts
             Payable, prepare the necessary adjusting entries to reclassify
             some accounts payable into its appropriate liability accounts;


          b) The Management determine the validity of  accounts  pertaining
             to the aid to barangays and terminal leave benefits and  cause
             the immediate payment or release of funds, as warranted; and


          c) The Management cause the reversion of  accounts  which  remain
             outstanding for over two (2) years and  for  which  no  actual
             claim has been filed, or which is  not  covered  by  perfected
             contracts on record, or for which the  purpose  of  the  trust
             fund has already been accomplished.

Recommendation:

    16. We recommended that Management:


          a) Conduct verification, analysis, and validation of the balances
             of the Due to NGAs accounts, determine the source agencies and
             the status of programs,  projects  and  activities  for  which
             funds were received; and  maintain  complete  records  of  the
             same;


          b) Direct the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the  balances  of
             Due to NGAs account with  the  balances  of  concerned  source
             agencies, and effect the necessary adjustments, as warranted;


          c) Make representations with identified source agencies  for  the
             return of unutilized balances related to completed projects;


          d) Make representations with the DA and the Department of  Budget
             and Management on  the  release  of  the  remaining  funds  in
             relation  to  the  completed  projects  under  the   DA-PAMANA
             Program; and


          e) Make representations with the source agencies on the necessary
             actions to be taken for funds received but for which  intended
             programs, projects, and activities remain unimplemented, or if
             implemented, were not completed or abandoned.

Recommendation:

    12. We recommended the following:


          a)  Management  established  strong  internal  controls  in   the
             handling of cash particularly the collections and  remittances
             from the  Collectors  to  the  Municipal  Treasurer,  and  the
             collections and deposits made by the  Municipal  Treasurer  to
             the  AGDB,  to  prevent  further  incurrence   of   unrecorded
             collections and/or over-deposit of collections, which resulted
             to abnormal/negative  balances  of  the  Cash  Local  Treasury
             account;


          b) The Municipal Accountant make representations  with  concerned
             government agencies relative to the  refund  of  erroneous  or
             over-remittances made. In case refund is not  granted  and  no
             other course of action can be taken, the Municipal  Accountant
             refund the said amounts;


          c) The Municipal  Accountant  exert  effort  in  identifying  the
             forwarded negative balances of Due to PhilHEALTH account under
             the General Fund and Economic Enterprise; and


          d) The Municipal Accountant properly monitor the withholding  and
             remittance  of  mandatory  contributions  so   as   to   avoid
             under/over-remittance of contributions; and review  thoroughly
             the schedule of payables due to concerned agencies during  the
             month  and  that  remittance  shall   be   based   on   actual
             contributions vis-a-vis payroll deductions.

Management submit the Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF to the Office of the Auditor duly certified by the Municipal Accountant on or before the 15th day after the end of each month pursuant to Item 5.1.5 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.

Complying and Implementing Items 6.0 and 7.0 of COA Circular No. 2012-004 dated November 28, 2012 with subject:  “Demand for Immediate Liquidation and Settlement of All Cash Advances outstanding as of December 31, 2011.”

We recommend the Inventory Committee to conduct annual physical inventory of all assets under PPE to ensure fairness of accounts presentation in the financial statements. In the preparation of the report, the prescribed format shall be used as provided in “Annex 49” of the same guidelines. Assignment of property number should be properly done so that each items has a unique code for easy reference. The output of the physical count of all inventories will determine the adjustment in the books of the agency and proper recording to reflect discrepancy/ies must be made thereof to present the correct carrying amount of these assets. Also, the GSO shall likewise prepare and maintain Stock and Property Cards for these assets using the Annex 36 and 37 format of the above-mentioned provision.

We further recommend to investigate balance of the Fuel, Oil and Lubricants Inventory account if this inventory held for consumption is still existing.
